decision: Consolidate instrument-computer experiments into one repo-native package and CLI.

choices.choice: Use python/scbe/instrument_computer.py as the reusable package boundary.
choices.reason: Keeps shared logic importable and avoids depending on scripts or C:/dev scratch files.

choices.choice: Keep scripts/audio/instrument_computer.py as a thin CLI.
choices.reason: Provides a single user-facing demo surface without mixing CLI code into package logic.

choices.choice: Expose both 8 primary faces and broad emit_all faces in Holophonor receipts.
choices.reason: The primary faces are traceable through tongue_isa; broad faces preserve the existing instrument fan-out.

choices.choice: Keep Haskell as an explicit primary face.
choices.reason: The CA stack model maps cleanly to pure functional stack transformations even though Haskell is less common in mainstream product deployment.

choices.choice: Wire STISTA via atomic_tokenization rows for each played op.
choices.reason: The repo has a real finite atomic-tokenization runtime; receipts should use that instead of treating STISTA as only prose.

choices.choice: Do not claim execution on every emitted language.
choices.reason: The local receipt executes Python; other faces are emitted unless external compilers are run separately.

choices.choice: Implement stateful shell RAM as old movie-player reels.
choices.reason: Segmenting the tape into reels gives a concrete mechanism for bounded tape chunks and automatic reel changes while preserving BF-class semantics.
